behavior: Hovers to feed, flies with rapid, precise movements, and catches small insects as well as taking nectar.
field_marks: Tiny, compact hummingbird with a long, slender, slightly downcurved dark bill.
Bright emerald to golden-green crown and upperparts.
Gray-white to whitish underparts, often with buffy flanks.
Adult male has an iridescent ruby-red throat that can look dark in poor light; female lacks it.
Short dark tail: forked in males; females show white tips on the outer tail feathers.
Large dark eye, very short legs, and long, narrow wings adapted for hovering.
habitat: Open woodlands and forest edges, including meadows, old fields, orchards, gardens, hedgerows, and scrub.

sources.title: Ruby-throated Hummingbird Identification — All About Birds
